message:
v0.3.5: decision-theoretic guard override, break policy rejection deadlock

Three fixes for remaining production issues on high-usage (>80%) machines:

1. Pressure-proportional guard penalty (policy.rs): Replace binary
   pressure_is_green with full PressureLevel tracking. The guard penalty
   in enforce_policy is now scaled by pressure severity:
   - Green: skip guard (no urgency)
   - Yellow: full penalty (standard)
   - Orange: penalty × 0.25 (urgency rising, tolerate uncertainty)
   - Red/Critical: penalty = 0 (bypass guard, survival mode)

   Decision-theoretic rationale: the expected loss of disk exhaustion
   (machine down, all agent sessions killed) vastly exceeds the expected
   loss of deleting a regenerable build artifact. At Orange+, the guard's
   uncertainty is dominated by the catastrophic downside of inaction.

   This breaks the death spiral where guard Fail → reject ALL candidates
   → disk fills → machine dies, observed on vmi1152480, vmi1153651,
   vmi1156319, vmi1227854 (all at 80-88% usage with 12-50 candidates
   found but 0 approved).

2. Unified pressure tracking: observe_window() now reads the stored
   pressure_level instead of taking a separate boolean parameter,
   simplifying the API and ensuring consistency.

3. Scan channel saturated rate-limit (loop_main.rs): Increased from 60s
   to 300s. Long scans (600s) with 60s monitor ticks produced 9 log
   messages per scan — now at most 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.4"
+version = "0.3.5"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
